channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Azerbaijani and U.S. officials suggest plane that crashed may have been hit by weapons fire
item.description: Russian air defense systems may have brought down an Azerbaijani airliner this week, a U.S. official said Friday after an Azerbaijani minister also suggested the plane was hit by a weapon, citing expert analysis and survivor accounts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli soldiers burn a hospital in Gaza
item.description: Israeli soldiers stormed and burned a hospital in isolated northern Gaza after forcibly removing staff and patients on Friday, Health Ministry officials said. The Israeli military said the hospital was being used by Hamas fighters as a base, although it did not provide evidence.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SEE IT: China stuns with maiden flight of sixth-generation aircraft
item.description: China appeared to unveil it's newest sixth-generation fighter aircraft after social media video and photos showed the tailless aircraft soaring above.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea's opposition-controlled National Assembly votes to impeach acting President Han
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s opposition-controlled National Assembly voted Friday to impeach acting President Han Duck-soo despite vehement protests by governing party lawmakers, further deepening the country’s political crisis set off by President Yoon Suk Yeol’s stunning imposition of m
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US sanctions the founder of Georgia's ruling political party
item.description: President Joe Biden has imposed sanctions on Bidzina Ivanishvili, the founder of Georgia’s ruling political party.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US teacher jailed in Russia is formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than four years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: NATO will step up Baltic Sea patrols as Finland probes the possible sabotage of undersea cables
item.description: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte says the military alliance will step up patrols in the Baltic Sea area as Finnish investigators work to establish whether a ship linked to Russia sabotaged undersea cables there this week.
item.dc:creator: Lorne Cook, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Azerbaijan Airlines blames deadly plane crash on 'external interference' as Russia speculation grows
item.description: Azerbaijan Airlines has suspended flights to multiple Russian airports in the wake of a deadly plane crash, which it attributed to "physical and technical external interference.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about South Korean acting President Han's impeachment
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The impeachment of South Korea's acting President Han Duck-soo Friday has plunged the country into further political turmoil, coming less than two weeks after lawmakers impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: China unveils world's largest amphibious warship
item.description: China has launched a new amphibious assault ship, named the Sichuan, which is capable of launching fighter jets and is designed to compete with U.S. military strength in the seas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese man sentenced to death for killing 35 people after driving into a crowd
item.description: A court in China has sentenced a man to death for killing 35 people last month by driving into crowd, in an attack that raised national concern about mass killings.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — The Chinese government placed sanctions on seven companies on Friday in response to recent U.S. announcements of military sales and aid to Taiwan, the self-governing island that China claims as part of its territory. The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea's opposition-controlled National Assembly votes to impeach acting President Han
item.description: South Korea’s opposition-controlled National Assembly has voted to impeach acting President Han Duck-soo.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel strikes Houthi rebels in Yemen's capital while the WHO chief says he was meters away
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — A new round of Israeli airstrikes in Yemen on Thursday targeted the Houthi rebel-held capital and multiple ports, while the World Health Organization's director-general said the bombardment occurred nearby as he prepared to board a flight in Sanaa, with a crew member injured.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Croatia's outspoken president is seeking reelection. He faces several contenders in Sunday's vote
item.description: ZAGREB, Croatia (AP) — Croatia’s left-leaning president, an outspoken critic of Western military support for Ukraine in its war against Russia, is running for reelection this weekend against an array of contenders, including the candidate backed by the conservative government.
item.dc:creator: SAŠA KAVIĆ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli attorney general orders probe into report that alleged Netanyahu's wife harassed opponents
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s attorney general has ordered police to open an investigation into Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s wife on suspicion of harassing political opponents and a witness in the Israeli leader’s corruption trial.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy defend Silicon Valley's foreign-born hires
item.description: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy, co-chairs of President-elect Trump’s new “Department of Government Efficiency,” are defending the tech industry’s reliance on foreign-born engineers as the incoming Trump administration prepares to crack down on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: NATO calls for investigation into Azerbaijan Airlines crash
item.description: NATO called for a formal investigation Thursday into the Azerbaijan Airlines plane crash that killed 38 people in Kazakhstan. “Our thoughts and prayers are with the families and victims of #AzerbaijanAirlines flight #J28243.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Russia cautions against 'hypotheses' in Azerbaijan Airlines crash
item.description: Moscow on Thursday warned against speculation that Russian air defenses may have caused the crash of an Azerbaijan Airlines plane in Kazakhstan this week that killed 38 people.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Taliban claims Pakistani airstrikes killed 46
item.description: A Taliban government official said the airstrikes Pakistan launched on Afghanistan Tuesday killed 46 people, including women and children, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu warns Houthis amid calls for Israel to wipe out terror leadership as it did with Nasrallah, Sinwar
item.description: Former Israeli officials discuss the possibility of Jerusalem targeting the Houthi leadership in the same way it took out other terror leaders from Hamas and Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO leaders predict era of 2% defense spending 'probably history' as Trump reportedly floats higher target
item.description: European leaders are making clear ahead of the Trump presidency that they plan to spend more on defense as Russia remains a top threat and as security concerns in the Middle East and North Africa escalate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Matt Petgrave, involved in play that led to Adam Johnson's death, asks for donations for legal costs
item.description: Hockey player Matt Petgrave has called the death of Adam Johnson “a tragic accident” in a public appeal for donations to his legal costs amid a police investigation.
item.dc:creator: KEN MAGUIRE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Christmas in Puerto Rico is a 45-day celebration with caroling, festive decorations, family feasts and more
item.description: Christmas in Puerto Rico is spent with loved ones singing carols, sitting around the table for a meal and exchanging gifts. There celebrations last from Thanksgiving to mid-January.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China says US is 'playing with fire' after latest military aid for Taiwan
item.description: U.S. military sales and assistance aim to help Taiwan defend itself and deter China from launching an attack.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Germans mourn the 5 killed and 200 injured in the apparent attack on a Christmas market
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Germans on Saturday mourned the victims of an apparent attack in which authorities say a doctor drove into a busy outdoor Christmas market, killing five people, injuring 200 others and shaking the public’s sense of security at what would otherwise be a time of joy.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, GEIR MOULSON and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, NewsNation
